scenario: A company uses several Power Apps canvas apps and Power Automate cloud flows. You create a Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policy that blocks one of the connectors used in the apps and flows. You apply the policy to the production environment.
q: For each statement, select Yes if it is true, otherwise No.

rows.s: Users will be able to run a published canvas app that is in violation of the DLP policy.
Cloud flows that are in violation of the DLP policy will be suspended.
Applying another DLP policy where the connector is classified as Business allows the apps and flows to run.

why: Users can NOT run a published canvas app that violates the DLP policy - they get a 'DLP conflict' error message (statement 1 = No). Cloud flows that violate a DLP policy ARE suspended - a background process automatically sets violating flows to the suspended state and they will not run (statement 2 = Yes). Applying another DLP policy that classifies the connector as Business does NOT fix it: DLP policies combine restrictively, so if ANY applicable policy blocks the connector combination, the apps/flows are still blocked (statement 3 = No).
